question_text: Which of the following best explains why sound cannot travel through a vacuum?

options.label: A
options.text: Sound waves are too weak to cross empty space

options.label: B
options.text: Sound requires particles to vibrate and transfer energy between them

options.label: C
options.text: Vacuum absorbs all sound energy immediately

options.label: D
options.text: Sound waves can only travel downwards through air

explanation: Sound is a mechanical wave that requires a medium (like air, water, or solid) with particles that can vibrate and pass energy to neighbouring particles. In a vacuum, there are no particles, so sound cannot propagate.
